text.title: Stratified sampling recipe — core rules
text.summary: Six testable rules every recipe must satisfy.

rule.statement: Quota per stratum = max(floor, proportional). Raw proportional sampling alone always drops rare strata to 0 once N is small; the floor (default 5 cases per non-empty stratum) is non-negotiable.
rule.rationale: Without a floor the daily slice systematically ignores low-volume cohorts (new tenants, new tools), where regressions land first.

rule.statement: Every recipe declares a single integer `seed`. The sampler is deterministic given (manifest, recipe, seed). Re-running the sample on the same inputs MUST yield the identical case-ids.
rule.rationale: Non-deterministic samples make day-over-day deltas un-debuggable — you cannot tell drift from sample variance.

rule.statement: A stratum's share of the daily slice MUST NOT exceed 40%, even if production traffic concentrates there. Cap excess on the head; redistribute to tail strata up to their `tail_floor` ceiling.
rule.rationale: One head intent dominating the slice masks tail regressions and turns the drift check into a single-intent canary.

rule.statement: The recipe is refreshed when traffic distribution shifts >10% absolute in any stratum's share, OR every 30 days, whichever comes first. Otherwise the prior recipe is carried forward verbatim.
rule.rationale: Refreshing every run thrashes case ids and breaks day-over-day diffability; never refreshing freezes the recipe against a stale traffic mix.

rule.statement: Every recipe declares `version` (semver), `created_at` (ISO 8601), and `parent_version` (the prior recipe id or null). New recipes that differ in quotas MUST bump minor; new strata bump major.
rule.rationale: Without versioning the eval harness cannot answer "what recipe produced yesterday's slice?" — a blocker for post-hoc forensic.

rule.statement: `N_daily` (total slice size) is fixed by budget, not by sum of per-stratum quotas. If proportional+floor over-shoots N_daily, scale the head down first; if it under-shoots, distribute leftover to tail strata.
rule.rationale: Letting quotas decide N_daily causes silent budget creep — the eval bill drifts up week over week.

rule.statement: All six rules satisfied: floor present, seed set, no head >40%, drift policy declared, version pinned, N_daily honoured.
rule.rationale: Aggregate positive conclusion for the decision tree.

rule.statement: Eval set ≤500 cases OR single stratum OR no traffic data — recipe overhead does not pay back.
rule.rationale: Aggregate negative conclusion for the decision tree.
